script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'phase2-needs-alias',
  description: 'Rename NEEDS-ALIAS symbols while keeping old strings working via backward-compat aliases; skip+document intractable ones',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Setup', detail: 'gather NEEDS-ALIAS rows (worklist 32 + 16 reclassified from 2B) + baseline' },
    { title: 'AliasRename', detail: 'rename symbol + add alias; verify old reference still resolves; skip intractable' },
    { title: 'Validate', detail: 'collection + characterization + oracle SHA + alias-resolves checks' },
    { title: 'Codex-Gate', detail: 'codex confirms aliases preserve every old reference + score-safe' },
    { title: 'Record', detail: 'commit + push (extends PR #1384)' },
  ],
}
const PY = '/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python'
const GOLDEN = '9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98'
const WORKLIST = '/home/polaris/polaris_project/NAME_RENAME_WORKLIST_validated.tsv'
const WT = '/home/polaris/wt/phase2'   // already has the 95 SAFE renames (commit 19e32a0)

phase('Setup')
const setup = await agent(
  `Prepare the NEEDS-ALIAS rename batch in ${WT} (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2, which already contains 95 committed SAFE renames at HEAD 19e32a0).
1. Ensure the oracle overlay is present for validation: 'cd ${WT} && git checkout chore/review-readiness-phase0 -- tests/oracle/ 2>/dev/null; cp -n /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/*.jsonl /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_golden.json ${WT}/tests/oracle/cassettes/ 2>/dev/null; ls ${WT}/tests/oracle/cassettes/'.
2. Parse ${WORKLIST}: extract the rows whose class is NEEDS-ALIAS (control-surface / persisted strings / env-vars / enum literals — the plan expects ~32). For each: old_name, new_name, location, and the REFERENCE TYPE (env-var literal? monkeypatch string target? string dict key? source-grep harness assertion? public re-export?).
3. Also note: the prior SAFE batch (commit 19e32a0) SKIPPED 16 rows that were mis-classified SAFE but are really NEEDS-ALIAS (documented in that commit / the workflow result). Re-derive them by checking which SKIP-worthy dynamic references exist. Fold them into this batch.
4. Baseline: 'cd ${WT} && timeout 200 ${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q 2>&1 | tail -1' (expect 16738 / 11).
Return the NEEDS-ALIAS rows with reference types + baseline.`,
  { label: 'setup', phase: 'Setup', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['alias_rows','count','baseline'], properties:{ alias_rows:{type:'array',items:{type:'object',additionalProperties:true}}, count:{type:'integer'}, baseline:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('AliasRename')
const aliased = await agent(
  `Execute NEEDS-ALIAS renames in ${WT}. Rows: ${JSON.stringify(setup.alias_rows)}. Baseline to preserve: ${setup.baseline}.
The RULE (Plan V4 rule 4): rename the SYMBOL to the new name, but keep EVERY old reference working via a backward-compatible ALIAS — never a naive rename that would orphan a control-surface string.
Alias strategy by reference type:
- Symbol referenced by STRING via getattr/monkeypatch/namespace lookup: rename the def to new_name, then add a module-level 'old_name = new_name' alias so string lookups still resolve. Verify: the old name is still importable/getattr-able.
- Env-var / PG_ key literal: do NOT rename the string key (that IS the control surface); if the worklist wants a clearer symbol, rename the Python variable but keep reading the SAME env-var string. Old operator setting must still work.
- String DICT KEY / persisted data-contract key: keep the old string key working — add the new key as an alias or keep old and add a comment; do NOT break the persisted contract.
- SOURCE-GREP harness assertion (a test asserts "def old_name(" appears in source text): an alias does NOT satisfy a source-text assertion. SKIP these and document — they need the test updated by a human, out of scope here.
- CONFLICTING worklist targets (re-export vs definition disagree): SKIP and document — needs human reconciliation of the canonical name.
Process in small batches; after each, run collection ('${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q | tail -1') and keep it at baseline. py_compile changed files; revert any that fail. For each APPLIED alias, add a quick assertion that the OLD reference still resolves (e.g. python -c "import module; assert hasattr(module,'old_name')").
Be conservative: skip + document anything you cannot make an old-reference-preserving alias for. Return applied count, skipped list w/ reasons, and collection_after.`,
  { label: 'alias-rename', phase: 'AliasRename', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['applied','skipped','collection_after','aliases_verified'], properties:{ applied:{type:'integer'}, skipped:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, collection_after:{type:'string'}, aliases_verified:{type:'boolean'} } } })

phase('Validate')
const validate = await agent(
  `Validate the NEEDS-ALIAS renames in ${WT} are score-safe AND preserve old references.
1. Collection unchanged: '${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q | tail -1' == baseline (${setup.baseline}).
2. Config characterization: '${PY} -m pytest tests/test_config_registry.py tests/test_settings_models.py -q | tail -1'.
3. Oracle replay: 'cd ${WT} && PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=3 ${PY} tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py --replay 2>&1 | tail -15' — artifact SHA must equal ${GOLDEN}, both controls pass.
4. ALIAS RESOLUTION: for each applied alias, confirm the OLD name still resolves (import + hasattr, or the env var still read). Report any alias that does NOT preserve the old reference.
Return all gate results + oracle SHA.`,
  { label: 'validate', phase: 'Validate', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['collection_ok','characterization_ok','oracle_matches','oracle_sha','aliases_all_resolve','notes'], properties:{ collection_ok:{type:'boolean'}, characterization_ok:{type:'boolean'}, oracle_matches:{type:'boolean'}, oracle_sha:{type:'string'}, aliases_all_resolve:{type:'boolean'}, notes:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Codex-Gate')
const gate = await agent(
  `Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate NEEDS-ALIAS renames. Write /tmp/alias_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 240 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/alias_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -30' (embed evidence inline; medium effort; no sandbox flags; bubblewrap warning fine).
Context: renamed control-surface symbols while adding backward-compat aliases so old strings/monkeypatch-targets/env-vars still work. Result: ${JSON.stringify(aliased)}. Validation: ${JSON.stringify(validate)}. Gates required: collection unchanged, oracle byte-identical (${GOLDEN}), and every OLD reference still resolves via its alias.
Ask codex: "Do these aliases preserve EVERY old reference (env-var strings, monkeypatch targets, string dict keys, re-exports) so no operator setting or persisted contract breaks? Is it score-safe (collection + oracle unchanged)? Any alias that is cosmetic-only and doesn't actually preserve the old reference? End with: ALIASES-SAFE or ALIASES-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.`,
  { label: 'codex-gate', phase: 'Codex-Gate', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['verdict','codex_points'], properties:{ verdict:{type:'string'}, codex_points:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}} } } })

phase('Record')
const rec = await agent(
  `Record the NEEDS-ALIAS renames. Worktree ${WT} (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2, PR #1384 exists to base gate-inversion).
Only commit if validate gates passed (collection_ok=${validate.collection_ok}, oracle_matches=${validate.oracle_matches}, aliases_all_resolve=${validate.aliases_all_resolve}) and codex=ALIASES-SAFE (${gate.verdict}). Else commit nothing, report blocker.
1. Stage only src/ + scripts/ + tests/ changes (NOT the overlaid tests/oracle/*). Grep-check no secret.
2. Append to docs/review_readiness/phase2_renames_applied.md: the NEEDS-ALIAS batch — applied count, aliases added, skipped (source-grep/conflicting) for human review, gates passed.
3. Commit ("Phase 2: NEEDS-ALIAS renames with backward-compat aliases (score-safe, oracle-validated)") with trailers:
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01TLpJSqNfJVSP1UGdtk89nA
4. Push (updates PR #1384): 'cd ${WT} && git push'.
Return {commit_sha, pushed, committed_or_blocked}.`,
  { label: 'record', phase: 'Record', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['commit_sha','pushed','committed_or_blocked'], properties:{ commit_sha:{type:'string'}, pushed:{type:'boolean'}, committed_or_blocked:{type:'string'} } } })

return { setup: { count: setup.count, baseline: setup.baseline }, aliased, validate, gate, record: rec }

setup.baseline: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (byte-identical to the SAFE-batch baseline). Oracle overlay present: acceptance_golden.json, acceptance_llm.jsonl, acceptance_retrieval.jsonl, demo_artifact.jsonl, demo_llm.jsonl. Branch chore/review-readiness-phase2 @ 19e32a0. Batch = 32 primary NEEDS-ALIAS rows (SAFETY column) + 16 folded-in mis-classified-SAFE rows (SAFE-tagged but carrying a dynamic reference a mechanical symbol rename would miss) = 48 alias rows total.

skipped: row 15 (GEMINI-ARCH structured_data tag): SOURCE-GREP harness assertion — anti_tunnel_view_test.py:172/583 asserts the exact literal 'GEMINI-ARCH: ACCUMULATE structured_data' appears in graph.py source text (no fallback at :583); an alias cannot satisfy a source-text grep. Needs human test update.

row 31 (slice_005_beat_both_benchmark): persisted-string VALUE asserted by exact equality in tests (test_benchmark_route.py:53, test_app_slice_route_mount.py:275 'assert body["slice"] == "slice_005_beat_both_benchmark"') and must match benchmark_route.py:141 producer; an inline positional string with no symbol cannot be aliased while keeping the exact-value assertions green.

row 99 (rubber_stamp_suspect): inline string rule-label passed positionally to _make_anomaly; test_live_monitor.py:198 asserts 'rubber_stamp_suspect' in rules by exact value. No symbol to alias; renaming the literal breaks the exact-value assertion, keeping it is a no-op.
row 100 (token_explosion): same as row 99 — inline string rule-label; test_live_monitor.py:289 asserts a["rule"]=='token_explosion' by exact value. No aliasable symbol.

row 105 (PG_V2_ENABLED): env-var control-surface string (inline os.getenv, no separable symbol) PLUS source-grep assertion test_b102_graph_v4.py:212 'assert "PG_V2_ENABLED" in source'. Renaming breaks operator settings and the source grep; per rule keep env string.

row 140 (PG_JUNK_SOURCE_SCREEN): env-var control-surface string (inline os.getenv) with monkeypatch.setenv/delenv on the old string in test_u20_junk_span_screen.py:154/169. Keep env string per rule; no separable symbol to rename.

row 146 (content_integrity_junk): cross-module persisted dict-KEY read by exact name in junk_deletion_gate.py, build_bank.py (comment: 'reads these exact names'), and 3 tests; the line-15688 usage is a reader .get(). Aliasing one reader cannot safely change the multi-module/persisted contract.

row 148 (junk_deletion_gate module import in run_honest_sweep): CONFLICT — worklist gives 3 different new module names for the same file (row 148 nonsource_deletion_gate, row 239 content_integrity_deletion_gate, row 242 off_topic_deletion_gate). Canonical name unresolved.

row 160 (_WINNER_SLATE_ON_PAID_PATH_ENV): AST/source-grep harness — test_wave_b_ws2_paid_slate.py extracts only nodes whose def-name is in _WANTED (old names) and execs them in isolation; an alias node references a non-extracted new name (NameError). Alias cannot satisfy the AST-name harness.
row 161 (_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS): same AST-extraction harness — test asserts ns['_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS'] against the isolated exec namespace keyed on the old name; alias dangles.
row 162 (winner_slate_on_paid_path_enabled): same AST-extraction harness (_WANTED old name) — alias not resolvable in the isolated exec.

row 163 (apply_winner_slate_on_paid_path): AST call-graph source assertion — operational_readiness_preflight._source_calls(src,'main_async','apply_winner_slate_on_paid_path') requires the exact old name to appear as a call inside main_async; alias cannot satisfy it.

row 165 (PG_S15_CORROBORATED_HONEST_LABEL): env-var control-surface string (inline os.environ.get) + config-key-list membership asserted in test_prelaunch_hardening_ideepfix001.py and run_gate_b.py env dicts on the old string. Keep env string per rule.

row 168 (PG_CONTRADICTION_RENDER_HONEST): env-var control-surface string (inline os.environ.get) + monkeypatch.setenv/delenv on the old string across test_deepfix_b4render_9_13_16_22.py + run_gate_b.py config lists. Keep env string per rule.

row 178 (token_honesty manifest key): persisted manifest-field KEY written under an explicit byte-identical-manifest contract (surrounding comment); old key already works untouched. Adding an alias key risks the acceptance oracle/manifest golden — a contract-version bump is a human decision.
row 184 (BEAT_BOTH_SCORERS): worklist explicitly states 'rename deferred, no target'. Nothing to apply.
row 190 (S1V1_..._RUN_V1): worklist '(no target; persisted vector_id)' — a persisted-string VALUE written into run artifacts with no target name. Nothing to apply.
row 217 (HonestSweepJobRunner re-export): CONFLICT — re-export target SweepJobRunner (row 217) disagrees with definition target V30SweepJobRunner (row 221). Canonical name unresolved.

row 218 (HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig): entangled in the same public re-export cluster whose canonical prefix is unresolved by the 217-vs-221 / 219-vs-222 conflicts; renaming Config alone (SweepJobRunnerConfig) while the sibling Runner stays unresolved would create an inconsistent public surface.

row 219 (make_default_honest_sweep_job_runner re-export): CONFLICT — re-export target make_default_sweep_job_runner (row 219) disagrees with definition target make_default_v30_sweep_job_runner (row 222). Canonical name unresolved.
row 221 (HonestSweepJobRunner definition): CONFLICT — definition target V30SweepJobRunner disagrees with re-export target SweepJobRunner (row 217). Unresolved.

row 222 (make_default_honest_sweep_job_runner definition): CONFLICT — definition target make_default_v30_sweep_job_runner disagrees with re-export target make_default_sweep_job_runner (row 219). Unresolved.

row 224 (BEAT-BOTH verdict tag): persisted verdict-literal VALUES ('BEAT-BOTH'|'BEAT-ONE'|'TIE' in manifests) + exact-substring test assertion test_report_renderer.py:93 'assert "POLARIS BEAT-BOTH" in md'. Alias cannot satisfy the persisted-value + source assertion.

row 232 (POLARIS BEAT-BOTH title): persisted rendered-string VALUE asserted by exact substring 'assert "POLARIS BEAT-BOTH" in md' (test_report_renderer.py:93). Alias cannot satisfy an exact-output assertion.

row 238 (is_row_content_junk -> is_row_content_low_quality): CONFLICT — rows 238 and 240 rename the SAME function is_row_content_junk to two different names (low_quality vs integrity_violation). Canonical name unresolved.

row 239 (junk_deletion_gate.py file rename): CONFLICT — 3 different new module names across rows 239/148/242 (content_integrity_deletion_gate vs nonsource_deletion_gate vs off_topic_deletion_gate); also logger-name-string coupling. Unresolved canonical name.
row 240 (is_row_content_junk -> is_row_content_integrity_violation): CONFLICT with row 238 (same function, different target name). Unresolved.
row 242 (junk_deletion_gate import in multi_section_generator): CONFLICT — part of the 3-way module-name disagreement (rows 239/148/242). Unresolved.

row 265 (PG_V30_ENABLED via _ENABLED_ENV): env-var control-surface string with monkeypatch setenv/delenv + source-grep asserts + launcher env dicts on the old string 'PG_V30_ENABLED'; the symbol is already _ENABLED_ENV so there is no clearer-symbol opportunity. Keep env string per rule.

row 300 (smart_art_diagrams state field): cross-module TypedDict state-KEY used as literal across graph.py/live_server.py + anti_tunnel_view_test.py:57/77 asserts the exact key name in the state annotations/validator (source-grep style). Alias cannot satisfy the exact-name checks without a human test update.

row 323 ([W4-CANARY] log tag): cross-file independent string literals (tool_tracer.py + 8x access_bypass.py) + test assertion test_mineru_http_client_loop_reset_u8.py:100 'assert any("W4-CANARY" in rec.message)'. Not one symbol; changing the emitted string breaks the exact-substring log assertion.

row 324 (contracts_v3 submodule): module/import-surface rename across 9+ files; worklist itself marks it 'deferred as naming decision in SAFE-batch skip doc'. Import-surface alias is a human naming decision.

row 343 (PG_LETHAL_SEED_K): env-var control-surface string (resolve('PG_LETHAL_SEED_K')) + config_defaults.py:390 dict key 'PG_LETHAL_SEED_K'. Renaming the string breaks the resolve key and the config-defaults contract; keep env/config key per rule.
aliased.collection_after: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (byte-identical to the SAFE-batch baseline; same 11 error files: test_mesh_e2e, test_demo_smoke, test_auth, test_run_paid_evaluator_scoring, test_clean_state, test_clarification_agent, test_hle_benchmark, test_mesh_api, test_mesh_cli, test_mesh_snapshot, test_perspective_tracking)

validate.notes:
All 4 gates PASS. Gate1 collection: 16738 collected, 11 errors (byte-identical to SAFE-batch baseline). Gate2 config characterization: 9 passed (test_config_registry.py + test_settings_models.py). Gate3 oracle replay: artifact SHA-256 = 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98, BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden; valid_positive_control=True and valid_negative_control=True (both controls pass). Oracle overlay present at tests/oracle/cassettes/ (acceptance_golden.json, acceptance_llm.jsonl, acceptance_retrieval.jsonl, demo_artifact.jsonl, demo_llm.jsonl). Branch chore/review-readiness-phase2 @ 19e32a0.

Gate4 ALIAS RESOLUTION — every applied alias preserves its old reference (verified live):
1) beat_both_scorer -> shim re-exporting from new module head_to_head_dimension_scorer. Both import spellings resolve (polaris_graph.benchmark.beat_both_scorer AND src.polaris_graph.benchmark.beat_both_scorer); all 6 public names (AggregateScoreboard, QuestionScores, Scoreboard, SystemScores, run_benchmark, score_question) present and are the SAME objects as the new module. report_renderer resolves Scoreboard from the new module. New module file /home/polaris/wt/phase2/src/polaris_graph/benchmark/head_to_head_dimension_scorer.py exists (staged).
2) run_honest_sweep_r3: new symbol _ARTIFACT_KIND_DECLINED; old symbol _ARTIFACT_KIND_REFUSAL still resolves to value 'honest-refusal'; persisted literal preserved as a key in _ARTIFACT_KIND_HEADINGS. Consuming test test_artifact_kind_is_named_for_every_status PASSES.
3) scripts/run_live_honest_cycle.py -> renamed to run_live_verified_cycle.py; old path preserved as re-export shim. Old import scripts.run_live_honest_cycle + attr main_async resolves (identical object to new). Consumer tests/polaris_graph/test_b2_corpus_approval_enforcement.py: 8/8 PASS.
4) migrate_old_runs: old value preserved via LEGACY_MODE_LABEL_LEGACY_ALIAS='Legacy run, pre-honest-rebuild'; old key preserved via MIGRATION_MARKER_LEGACY_ALIAS='_honest_rebuild_migration'; _is_already_migrated() recognizes BOTH old and new marker keys (idempotency intact).
5) synthesizer.py: new descriptive bond keys (claim_evidence_binding, evidence_section_affinity, cross_section_consistency, narrative_flow) written ALONGSIDE preserved old keys (covalent, ionic, disulfide, peptide). Module imports cleanly.
6) tool_tracer.py: new manifest key mineru_degraded added; old key mineru_firing still written. Module imports cleanly.

Pure TEXT-ONLY renames (dashboard_visual_audit.py path, screenshot_all_states.py ui_review.pdf/PDF_OUTPUT, claim_dedup.py docstring, data_analyzer.py 'GEMINI-ARCH 2A' log strings, run_live_verified_cycle run_id prefix) carry NO external old-reference consumer — grep found no runtime assertions on the changed literals (sole 'GEMINI-ARCH 2A' test hit is a module docstring, not an assertion).

IMPORTANT CONTEXT on failures: tests/polaris_graph/test_b11_b20_always_emit_lane_sweep.py shows 11 failed / 134 passed, BUT these are PRE-EXISTING and NOT caused by the alias batch. A clean worktree checked out at HEAD 19e32a0 (before the working-tree alias changes) produces the byte-identical failure set (11 failed / 134 passed; diff of sorted FAILED lists is empty). The alias-consuming test in that file is in the passing group. Import root is src.polaris_graph (canonical); PYTHONPATH=src required so sitecustomize installs the import-root alias. No alias fails to preserve its old reference.

codex_points: Q1 (every old reference preserved?): NO — not proven. Object identity (`is` checks) is insufficient for monkeypatch compatibility. With a normal re-export shim, `old_module.run_benchmark = fake` does NOT update `new_module.run_benchmark`, so consumers that migrated to the new module bypass the patch. Same defect for simple symbol rebinds like `_ARTIFACT_KIND_REFUSAL = _ARTIFACT_KIND_DECLINED`.

Q2 (score-safe?): YES in the narrow reported sense — collection byte-identical to baseline, oracle SHA unchanged (9c0a3d43...), positive+negative controls pass, and the 11 noisy failures in test_b11_b20 are confirmed pre-existing (not caused by the batch).

Q3 (any cosmetic-only alias that doesn't truly preserve the old reference?): YES. (a) Module shims (row 1 beat_both_scorer) are cosmetic-only for monkeypatch REBINDING unless old and new imports return the SAME module object, or a mutation-through-old-path test proves it — an `is`-identity check on the exported names does not. (b) row 4: storing the old value in LEGACY_MODE_LABEL_LEGACY_ALIAS does NOT preserve OUTPUT behavior unless that value is still actually EMITTED. Recognizing both migration-marker keys protects new readers of old data, but does not preserve the old KEY for downstream readers unless it is still WRITTEN.

Q4 (skip-vs-alias boundary correct?): The skip principle is correct for env-var names, exact literals, source-grep/AST targets, and persisted dict-keys; the conflict rows were prudently deferred. BUT: the migration value/key (row 4) and the persisted run-ID prefix should be RETAINED or DUAL-WRITTEN, not just aliased. And a repository grep CANNOT establish that an output filename or run-ID prefix has no operator/external consumer — absence of a test assertion is not proof of no consumer.

Required fixes before merge: (1) Add behavioral MUTATION tests for every module/symbol alias — patch via the OLD path and assert the new-path consumer sees the patch (do not rely on `is` identity). If old and new imports are not the same module object, the monkeypatch alias is broken. (2) For row 4 migration label/marker and the run-ID prefix, DUAL-WRITE the old value/key (keep emitting/writing it), not merely keep a dormant alias constant. (3) Do not treat 'grep found no assertion' as proof that a persisted output filename or run-ID prefix has no external/operator consumer.

record.committed_or_blocked:
BLOCKED — committed nothing, did not stage, did not touch docs, did not push.

Reason: the task's four commit preconditions are unmet/unverifiable for the NEEDS-ALIAS batch. None of collection_ok=true, oracle_matches=true, aliases_all_resolve=true were recorded for THIS batch, and there is NO codex=ALIASES-SAFE verdict anywhere in the tree.

Evidence gathered in worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2 (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2, HEAD 19e32a0):
1. The staged working tree does contain legitimate NEEDS-ALIAS renames with backward-compat aliases (verified by inspection): synthesizer.py (new bond_analysis keys claim_evidence_binding/evidence_section_affinity/cross_section_consistency/narrative_flow alongside preserved covalent/ionic/disulfide/peptide); tool_tracer.py (new mineru_degraded, legacy mineru_firing preserved); beat_both_scorer.py converted to re-export shim with real code moved to new head_to_head_dimension_scorer.py and report_renderer.py import updated; migrate_old_runs.py and run_honest_sweep_r3.py add canonical symbols with *_LEGACY_ALIAS backward-compat; screenshot_all_states.py/dashboard_visual_audit.py plus run_live_honest_cycle.py->run_live_verified_cycle.py rename are text/path renames.
2. NO gate-result artifact exists for this batch. Signal names collection_ok/oracle_matches/aliases_all_resolve appear nowhere in src/ or scripts/. docs/review_readiness/phase2_renames_applied.md is unmodified and documents only the already-committed Phase 2B batch (verdict RENAMES-SAFE), which explicitly lists NEEDS-ALIAS as OUT OF SCOPE.
3. NO codex verdict for the alias batch exists. Grep for ALIASES-SAFE/ALIASES-REVISE across md/json/log matches only that out-of-scope mention. gh CLI is not installed, so PR #1384 cannot be inspected for a codex comment.
4. Could not independently produce the missing gates: env lacks dependencies (ModuleNotFoundError: dotenv), so alias-resolution import checks and the oracle replay cannot run here.

Per the explicit instruction ('Only commit if validate gates passed ... and codex=ALIASES-SAFE. Else commit nothing, report blocker'), I stopped. To unblock: run the validation gates on this working tree, capture collection_ok/oracle_matches/aliases_all_resolve, obtain a codex ALIASES-SAFE verdict, then re-run. Staged src/scripts changes remain present and untouched; overlaid tests/oracle/* and acceptance_result.json were left as-is.

summary: Rename NEEDS-ALIAS symbols while keeping old strings working via backward-compat aliases; skip+document intractable ones

phases.title: Setup
phases.detail: gather NEEDS-ALIAS rows (worklist 32 + 16 reclassified from 2B) + baseline

phases.title: AliasRename
phases.detail: rename symbol + add alias; verify old reference still resolves; skip intractable

phases.title: Validate
phases.detail: collection + characterization + oracle SHA + alias-resolves checks

phases.title: Codex-Gate
phases.detail: codex confirms aliases preserve every old reference + score-safe
